agreements.FieloPLT__Agreement__c:
Please check below a suggestion of topics which you can explore under the terms and conditions (the “T&C”) of an incentive program (the “Program”).

Kindly note the terms “you”, “You” or “Company” as used herein refer to Fielo’s customer interested in learning about the T&C of an incentive program; and the terms “Party” or “Parties” refer to the existing parties under such incentive program.

1. Parties of the Program.

1.1 Company – The T&C shall include information about the Company or the corporate group which is in charge for / promoting the Program.

1.2 Participant / Member - For whom is the program designed for? The T&C may describe the requirements and the rules applying for the eligibility of an individual in order for such person to become a ‘Participant’ / a ‘Member’ under the Program.

1.3 Other Parties - Company and Participant are usually the main Parties under an incentive program, but it does not mean they are the only ones. It might be the case (depending on each Program) there are other Parties such as vendors, providers, customers, etc. In these cases, it is advisable to include information, requirements, and rules applying to such Parties as well.

2. Program Overview.

2.1 Scope of the Program – This item should give a general overview of the Program goals, its incentives, and how such incentives will be provided. Further information can be included as you believe convenient.

2.2 Territory – You may want to define under the T&C the countries, states or cities, (the “Territory”) where the Program shall be considered valid.

2.3 Enrollment / Registering – The T&C shall bring information about the rules and procedures applicable for an eligible individual to register as a Participant under the Program.

2.4 Incentives - What kind of incentives will the Program provide to the Participants? It is advisable to specify if it will be, for example, commissions, vouchers, special discounts, rebates, sales spiffs, promotions, etc.

2.5 Incentive Calculations and Payment - Once defined the kind of incentive existing under the Program, the Parties should know what are the parameters to calculate and provide it to the Participant. You may include here additional requirements for the Participant to comply with in order to receive the payment (for example, a “Good Standing Policy”).

2.6 Taxes – The T&C may indicate for the benefit of the Participant what are the taxes applying under the Program and which Party is in charge of collecting them.

2.7 Disputes / Claims = In case your Program is designed to have a relevant number of Participants, you may consider having an item under the T&C dedicated to Claims and other Disputes. Such item may include the criteria, rules, and procedures which a Participant shall follow in order to discuss that subject with you.

3. Term and Termination.

3.1 Term – You may choose between having a limited and defined-term Program, or an undefined one. It depends on your Program goals.

3.2 Modification of the T&C – You may reserve to Company the right to terminate, amend, suspend, modify or update the T&C of the Program, at any time and in its sole discretion, with no liability and no penalty due to such actions.

3.3 Termination of the Program; Disqualification – You may also reserve to Company the right to (i) terminate the Program; or (ii) revoke a Participant's account, and/or terminate Participant’s participation in the Program in its entirety. And just like the item before, Company may exercise those rights at any time and in its sole discretion, with no liability and no penalty.

3.4 Effects of Termination - Upon disqualification of a Participant or termination of the Program for any reason, Company may request Participant to immediately cease the use of all Company’s brochures, literature, documentation, and other materials within the Participant control, if any, and requires the immediate return of such materials to Company. Other effects might be included at your convenience.

4. Confidentiality.

You may requires Participant to keep as confidential all the incentives received, and also the Company information to which Participant might have access during the Program.

5. Indemnification, Liability, and Disclaimers.

It is advisable, and you may include in the T&C, what shall be the Parties' liability under the Program (including any limitation of it), and also, the Party’s indemnification rights and obligations to each other, and to third parties.

6. Representations and Warranties.

The company may request each Participant to make representations and warranties as a requirement to participate in the Program, such as (i) Participant is over the age of eighteen (18) and have the power and authority to enter into and perform obligations under the Program; (b) Participant will comply with the T&C; (c) Participant have provided accurate and complete registration information, including its legal name, address or telephone number, etc.

7. Intellectual Property Rights.

In case the participation of the Participant in the Program includes the granting of any rights to, or in, patents, copyrights, database rights, trade secrets, trade names, trademarks, or licenses (whether registered or unregistered), you may wish to include a provision in the T&C to regulate and limit the use of such Intellectual Property Rights.

8. Anti-corruption and Business Conduct

If Company maintains an anti-corruption policy, including but not limited to a Business Code of Conduct, for example, Company may request the Participant to know and to comply with such policy.

9. Governing Law and Jurisdiction/Arbitration

You may elect the governing law which shall apply and govern the T&C, and also, the courts and Jurisdiction under which any dispute arising out of the T&C or related to the Program shall be submitted to.

10. Relationship of the Parties.

It might be of your interest (for the benefit of the Company) to include in the T&C a provision confirming there is no employer-employee relationship, joint venture, partnership, or agency created by the Program, and Participant has no authority to act for, or on behalf of Company, and is not authorized to bind Company in any manner whatsoever.

11. Force Majeure.

You may include a Force Majeure provision in the T&C to state if there will be a liability for any of the Parties in case either one fails or delay in performance of its obligations under the Program because of circumstances beyond its reasonable control.

12. Notices

You shall consider including a paragraph under the T&C with instructions to Participants on how they shall communicate with Company to talk about the Program matters (for example, an e-mail addressed to [---]).

DISCLAIMER

The information provided by Fielo under this Guideline is for general information purposes only and it is not a substitute for professional advice. All information contained herein is provided in good faith, however, we make no representation or warranty of any kind, express or implied, regarding the accuracy, adequacy, validity, reliability, availability, or completeness of any information on this Guideline. Accordingly, before taking any action based on such information, we encourage you to consult with appropriate professionals.

Under no circumstance shall we have any liability to you for any loss or damage of any kind incurred as a result of the use of this Guideline or reliance on any information provided by it. Your use of the Guideline and your reliance on any information of it is solely at your own risk.
